sn7.18:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
sn7.18:1.2: Then a large number of firewood-gathering youths—students of a certain brahman of the Bhāradvāja clan—went to the forest grove. On arrival, they saw the Blessed One sitting in the grove—his legs folded crosswise, his body set straight, mindfulness established to the fore. On seeing him, they went to the brahman of the Bhāradvāja clan and, on arrival, said to him,
sn7.18:1.4: Gotama the contemplative is in that grove over there, sitting with his legs folded crosswise, his body set straight, mindfulness established to the fore.”
sn7.18:1.5: So the brahman of the Bhāradvāja clan, together with the youths, went to the forest grove.
sn7.18:1.6: On arrival, he saw the Blessed One sitting in the grove—his legs folded crosswise, his body set straight, mindfulness established to the fore.
sn7.18:1.7: On seeing him, he went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, addressed him in verse:
sn7.18:2.1: “In the deep-looking forest, teeming with terrors,
sn7.18:2.2: having plunged into the wilderness —desolate, empty—
sn7.18:2.3: unflinchingly, steadfastly, compellingly, you practice jhāna, monk:
sn7.18:3.1: Where no song is sung, where no music is played,
sn7.18:3.2: alone in the wilderness: the forest-dwelling sage.
sn7.18:3.4: that you live alone in the forest with rapturous mind.
sn7.18:4.2: for the three heavens unexcelled, in the company of the ruling lord of the worlds,
sn7.18:4.3: that, staying here in the wilderness, desolate, you practice austerities
sn7.18:5.1: The Buddha: “Whatever the longings or delights attached—always—
sn7.18:5.3: or yearnings born from the root of unknowing:
sn7.18:5.4: I’ve destroyed them all, down to the root.
sn7.18:6.2: with purified vision with regard to all things,
sn7.18:6.4: practice jhāna hidden from Brahmā, matured.”
